auth_config_env: Armeria TLS keystore (opt-in, plain HTTP by default)
Storage-backend egress only: ES_USERNAME/ES_PASSWORD, CASSANDRA_*, MYSQL_*, RABBIT_*
misconfig_patterns: STORAGE_TYPE=mem (default) — Docker quickstart left as production
9411 NodePort'd via stale firewall rule (legacy Spring Boot/Istio/Dapr deploys)
ES storage backend on same host unauth — full jaeger/zipkin index readable
Spring Boot Actuator co-exposed (/actuator/env, /actuator/heapdump pulls creds)
RabbitMQ 15672 management UI guest:guest perennial finding

active_probe.false_positive_check: Content-Type application/json + body is bare JSON array of strings (length>=1 = populated); body_not_contains '"data":' (Jaeger envelope) or '<html'

deployment_tells: /health JSON exposes storage backend class: InMemoryStorage{} | ElasticsearchStorage{...} | CassandraStorage{...} | MySQLStorage{...}
/api/v2/services returns bare JSON array (NOT {data:...} envelope — Jaeger discriminator)
pivot_paths: /api/v2/traces → spans with http.url + db.statement + error tags (secrets-in-tags common)
Shadow port 9200 Elasticsearch → raw zipkin* index (the prize)
/actuator/env → environment variables incl. DB connection strings
vulnerabilities: Zipkin-product: ZERO published GHSAs (auth-on-default thesis — no auth to patch)
CVE-2021-38153 — bundled Kafka client timing attack
sources: CANDIDATE — zipkin.io/pages/architecture.html ("no built-in authentication in the UI")
